verdict:
2026-07-01 19:38:37 PDT
Sound approach. My AC sketch would be exactly the approved small shape: keep `requireIdentity` as the gate, catch `resolveUid()` failures, log one stderr line with `[server] app-context rejected: ${reason}`, derive `reason` only from `AppContextError.message` with a safe fallback for unexpected errors, leave the client-facing 401/503 contracts unchanged, and cover the reason/redaction/response/503-silent cases with focused server tests. After reading `git log main..HEAD`, `git diff main...HEAD`, the full changed files, and the package manifests, the implementation matches that shape. It does not add a dependency, duplicate framework behavior, introduce a new abstraction, or hand-roll a larger logging subsystem for a single diagnostic line. No high-leverage approach concerns.
